description:
You are a data engineer who has just executed an overnight ETL job.  
    The job wrote its runtime log to  

        /home/user/etl/logs/pipeline.log

    Your manager only needs one very quick metric:  
    “How many ERROR events occurred during the latest run?”

    1. Read / parse the log stored at the path above. An ERROR event is any log
       line that contains the exact, uppercase word “ERROR” surrounded by one
       leading and one trailing blank space (␠ERROR␠).  
       Example pattern that should match:  
           “[2023-05-12 08:00:02] ERROR Source connection failure”

    2. Compute the total number of such ERROR lines found in the file.

    3. Write the result to  

        /home/user/etl/reports/error_count.txt

       • The file must contain exactly one line, no extra leading or trailing
         blank lines or spaces.  
       • The line must follow the precise key–value format below (all caps,
         equal sign, no spaces):  

             ERROR_COUNT=<N>

         where <N> is the integer count you obtained in step 2.

    4. Do not delete or modify the original log file.

    Automated tests will:
        • Verify the file exists at the exact path given.
        • Check that it contains exactly one line in the format above.
        • Confirm that the integer value matches the true count of ERROR
          entries present in /home/user/etl/logs/pipeline.log.

    You may accomplish everything with a single correctly-crafted shell
    command, but you may choose any approach that respects the constraints
    above.  
    Display the command’s console output (if any) so the user can see what was
    run, and then exit.
truth:
Initial filesystem state:
        Directory /home/user/etl/logs already exists (permissions 755).
        Directory /home/user/etl/reports already exists (permissions 755).

    Pre-existing file:
/home/user/etl/logs/pipeline.log  (permissions 644, LF line endings)
---------------------------------------------------------------------
[2023-05-12 08:00:01] INFO  Start extraction
[2023-05-12 08:00:02] ERROR Source connection failure
[2023-05-12 08:05:31] INFO  Retrying extraction
[2023-05-12 08:05:45] INFO  Extraction succeeded
[2023-05-12 08:06:00] INFO  Start transformation
[2023-05-12 08:06:12] ERROR Null value encountered
[2023-05-12 08:07:00] INFO  Transformation succeeded
[2023-05-12 08:07:15] INFO  Start load
[2023-05-12 08:07:45] ERROR Constraint violation on load
[2023-05-12 08:08:02] INFO  Load finished
---------------------------------------------------------------------

    Ground-truth value for automated verification:
        • Total ERROR lines (matching the “␠ERROR␠” rule) = 3

    Expected file after successful completion:
/home/user/etl/reports/error_count.txt  (permissions 644, LF line endings)
---------------------------------------------------------------------
ERROR_COUNT=3
---------------------------------------------------------------------
